audit_items.proof_trace: blstrs::G1Point::from_compressed accepts the identity point (point at infinity) as a valid compressed G1 element, returning Some(G1Point::identity()) rather than None. The code at L77-80 propagates this without explicit identity validation, allowing identity commitments to pass verification.
audit_items.attack_scenario: Attacker sends blob with commitment=0xc000...0000 (identity point). deserialize_compressed_g1 succeeds, verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch proceeds with identity commitment. Pairing checks may accept trivial proofs since e(O,g2)=1 for identity O, bypassing cryptographic guarantees.
